meta.name: neobankbeat
meta.description: Open-source directory of verified-active neobanks: traditional, hybrid fiat+crypto, and web3-native self-custodial money apps.

field_notes.category: traditional = licensed fiat, custodial · hybrid = fiat + custodial crypto · web3-native = self-custodial, on-chain-first
field_notes.rates: cashback/yield are 'up to' figures that change constantly and vary by region — always confirm with the issuer
field_notes.verification: defunct neobanks and pure BaaS/infrastructure are excluded; unverified fields are null rather than guessed

entities.name: Chime
entities.note: Largest US neobank; 20M+ members; SpotMe overdraft.
entities.story: Built to end overdraft fees for paycheck-to-paycheck America; grew on two-day-early wage access and fee-free SpotMe.

entities.name: Varo
entities.note: First US consumer fintech with a national bank charter.
entities.story: Spent three years and ~$100M to become the first US consumer fintech granted its own national bank charter.

entities.name: Current
entities.note: Everyday + teen banking; in-app crypto trading.
entities.story: Started as a teen debit card, expanded to full-spectrum banking for Americans living paycheck to paycheck.

entities.name: SoFi
entities.note: Bank charter; launched SoFiUSD stablecoin Dec 2025.
entities.story: From Stanford alumni-funded student loans to a chartered digital bank; issued SoFiUSD stablecoin in Dec 2025.
entities.founders: Mike Cagney, Dan Macklin, James Finnigan, Ian Brady

entities.name: Ally Bank
entities.story: Born from GMAC's 2009 rebrand into a branchless bank; proof digital-only could scale before the fintech era.

entities.name: Dave
entities.story: Pitched as the bear that fights the banks: cash advances against overdrafts, then a full spending account.

entities.name: One
entities.story: Walmart's play to own everyday banking for its 150M weekly shoppers; BNPL + debit + high-yield savings in one app.

entities.name: Step
entities.note: Teen banking + credit building, no fees.
entities.story: Teen banking that builds credit history before 18 via a secured spend card.

entities.name: Till Financial
entities.note: Family banking for kids' smart spending.

entities.name: GoHenry
entities.note: Kids' money app; acquired by Acorns.
entities.story: UK parents crowdfunded a kids' money app that taught a generation pocket-money-by-chores.

entities.name: Mercury
entities.story: Startup banking that absorbed half of YC after SVB collapsed; added IO cards and personal banking.

entities.name: Brex
entities.story: Two Brazilian teens who sold a payments company before 20 rebuilt corporate cards around startup underwriting.

entities.name: Ramp
entities.story: Spend management that markets itself on saving companies money; among the fastest-growing SaaS-fintechs ever.
entities.founders: Eric Glyman, Karim Atiyeh, Gene Lee

entities.name: Grasshopper
entities.note: Chartered digital bank for startups + SMBs.

entities.name: Karat
entities.story: Underwrites creators on audience data instead of FICO.

entities.name: Branch
entities.note: Instant pay + wallets for gig/hourly workers.

entities.name: Panacea Financial
entities.story: Founded by two physicians who couldn't get loans during residency; banking for doctors, dentists and vets.

entities.name: Purple
entities.note: Banking for people with disabilities; ABLE-friendly.
entities.story: Banking for people with disabilities designed not to endanger ABLE benefit eligibility.

entities.name: True Link
entities.story: Prepaid cards with guardrails protecting seniors from fraud and financial abuse.

entities.name: Charlie
entities.story: Banking for the 62+ crowd: early Social Security access and fraud protection over rewards points.

entities.name: Greenwood
entities.note: Digital banking for Black + Latino communities.
entities.story: Named for Tulsa's Black Wall Street; digital banking recirculating capital in Black and Latino communities.
entities.founders: Ryan Glover, Killer Mike, Andrew Young

entities.name: MoCaFi
entities.note: Closing the racial wealth gap via public-sector rails.
entities.story: Ex-JPMorgan banker's answer to Ferguson: closing the racial wealth gap via public-sector disbursement rails.

entities.name: Majority
entities.note: $5.99/mo membership banking for migrants; no SSN needed.
entities.story: Membership banking for migrants: no SSN required, community advisors, bundled calling + remittance.

entities.name: Zolve
entities.note: US credit for arriving immigrants (IN→US corridor).
entities.story: Credit cards for Indians landing in the US, underwritten on home-country data before they have a FICO.

entities.name: Comun
entities.note: Banking for Latino immigrants; passport onboarding.
entities.story: Built by Mexican founders for Latino immigrants: passport onboarding and fast remittance.

entities.name: Ellevest
entities.note: Women-first investing platform with banking roots.
entities.story: Wall Street veteran Sallie Krawcheck's bet that investing designed around women's pay curves and lifespans closes the gender money gap.

entities.name: First Women's Bank
entities.note: Women-founded, women-led chartered bank for women-owned SMBs.
entities.story: The first US bank founded by women, led by women, focused on lending to women-owned businesses.

entities.name: KOHO
entities.note: Canada's leading prepaid neobank; credit building.

entities.name: Neo Financial
entities.story: SkipTheDishes founders' second act: cashback-network banking from Calgary.

entities.name: Wealthsimple
entities.story: Canada's index-investing evangelist turned full-stack money app with crypto.

entities.name: Monzo
entities.story: The hot-coral card that made banking a brand; born from the Mondo prepaid beta and a record 96-second crowdfund.

entities.name: Starling Bank
entities.story: Anne Boden left a 30-year banking career at 54 to build the UK's first consistently profitable neobank.

entities.name: Zopa Bank
entities.story: Invented P2P lending in 2005, then traded the model for a full bank licence.

entities.name: ANNA Money
entities.note: SME banking with AI admin assistant.

entities.name: Monese
entities.note: No-credit-history onboarding; consumer app scaled back to B2B (XYB).
entities.story: An Estonian founder rejected by UK banks built no-credit-history onboarding for migrants; consumer app later folded into B2B platform XYB.

entities.name: Pockit
entities.note: Accounts for the underbanked; absorbed Monese users.
entities.story: Accounts for the UK's financially excluded; absorbed Monese's consumer base.

entities.name: Tandem Bank
entities.story: Pivoted from challenger #2 to a green lender financing home decarbonisation.

entities.name: Algbra
entities.story: Shariah-compliant, values-based finance for the two billion people underserved on ethics.

entities.name: Kestrl
entities.story: Muslim money app helping users grow wealth without interest.

entities.name: N26
entities.note: 8M+ customers; crypto via partner in some markets.
entities.story: Berlin's minimalist bank that scaled across the EU, retreated from UK/US, and finally reached profitability.

entities.name: bunq
entities.story: Ali Niknam bootstrapped the Netherlands' first new banking licence in 35 years with his own money.

entities.name: Tomorrow
entities.story: Hamburg social business: card spend funds climate projects.
entities.founders: Inas Nureldin, Jakob Berndt, Michael Schweikart

entities.name: Kontist
entities.note: Freelancer banking with auto tax buckets.
entities.story: Freelancer banking that auto-reserves German taxes in real time.

entities.name: Qonto
entities.story: Europe's SME banking leader out of Paris; absorbed Penta to own the German market too.

entities.name: Sumeria (Lydia)
entities.note: France's P2P leader turned full account.
entities.story: France's 'send me a Lydia' P2P verb rebranded to Sumeria to become a real current account.

entities.name: Nickel
entities.note: Open an account at the tabac; 4M+ across FR/ES/BE/PT/DE.
entities.story: Opened accounts through 6,500 tobacco shops — banking where French banks refused to go.

entities.name: Hype
entities.note: One of Italy's largest neobanks (Banca Sella).

entities.name: Tinaba
entities.note: Italian money app with Banca Profilo.

entities.name: Openbank
entities.note: Santander's digital bank; now US/MX too.

entities.name: Trade Republic
entities.note: Broker with full bank licence; custodial crypto.
entities.story: Berlin broker that pays ECB rate on cash and hands out 1% saveback — a bank disguised as a savings plan.
entities.founders: Christian Hecker, Thomas Pischke, Marco Cancellieri

entities.name: Wise
entities.story: Two Estonians tired of hidden FX fees built mid-market-rate transfers; now moving £100B+ a year.

entities.name: Klarna
entities.note: BNPL giant with full banking accounts.
entities.founders: Sebastian Siemiatkowski, Niklas Adalberth, Victor Jacobsson

entities.name: Papara
entities.story: Istanbul's fintech champion: 17M users and an M&A run across Turkey and Pakistan.

entities.name: Monobank
entities.story: Built by the ex-PrivatBank team; kept Ukraine's payments running through blackouts and full-scale war, with a cat mascot.

entities.name: T-Bank
entities.note: Ex-Tinkoff; 40M+ customers, one of the largest standalone neobanks.
entities.story: Tinkov built the world's largest standalone digital bank from a credit-card mailer; sanctions forced his exit.

entities.name: Kaspi
entities.story: Turned a distressed Kazakh bank into a super-app processing most of the country's payments and e-commerce.

entities.name: Nubank
entities.note: 131M customers (FY25); custodial crypto; US OCC conditional approval.
entities.story: Started after David Vélez's kafkaesque São Paulo branch visit; now Latin America's most valuable financial institution with 100M+ customers.
entities.licence: Bank licences BR/MX/CO; conditional US OCC approval (2026)
entities.founders: David Vélez, Cristina Junqueira, Edward Wible

entities.name: Ualá
entities.story: Buenos Aires historian-turned-founder set out to bank every Argentine teenager; now a regional multi-licence player.
entities.licence: Bank licences (AR via Wilobank, MX via ABC Capital)

entities.name: Stori
entities.story: Credit cards for the 60% of Mexicans banks reject; unicorn built on inclusion economics.

entities.name: PicPay
entities.note: Brazil's largest payments app; crypto + BRL stable rails.

entities.name: Brubank
entities.note: First licensed digital bank in Argentina.

entities.name: Lulo Bank
entities.note: Colombia's first fully digital licensed bank.

entities.name: KakaoBank
entities.story: Launched from Korea's dominant messenger; 300K accounts in 24 hours, profitable within two years.

entities.name: Toss Bank
entities.story: A dentist-turned-founder failed eight startups before Toss; the bank arm now anchors Korea's super-app.

entities.name: K bank
entities.note: Korea's first internet-only bank; Upbit ties.
entities.story: Korea's first internet bank; balance sheet supercharged by being Upbit's banking partner.

entities.name: WeBank
entities.note: Tencent-backed; world's largest digital bank by users.
entities.story: The world's largest digital bank: 400M+ customers on Tencent rails.

entities.name: Jupiter
entities.note: Indian neobank on Federal Bank rails.

entities.name: slice
entities.note: Merged with a small finance bank.
entities.story: Card-first startup that pulled off the rare feat of merging into a licensed Indian bank.

entities.name: Mahila Money
entities.story: From the SHEROES women's community to credit and accounts for women entrepreneurs in Bharat.

entities.name: Oraan
entities.story: Digitized Pakistan's women-led savings committees (ROSCAs) into a regulated product.

entities.name: bKash
entities.story: 'bKash me' is a verb in Bangladesh: 70M users, the reference case for mobile money outside Africa.

entities.name: GoTyme Bank
entities.licence: PH digital bank licence (Tyme x Gokongwei)

entities.name: Cake
entities.story: Digital bank inside Vietnam's ride-hailing app Be; 5M users in four years.

entities.name: GXBank
entities.note: Grab-led; Malaysia's first digital bank live.

entities.name: Ryt Bank
entities.story: Malaysia's AI-first bank: an LLM assistant as the primary interface.

entities.name: Minna Bank
entities.story: Japan's first digital-native bank, built cloud-first.

entities.name: ZA Bank
entities.note: First HK virtual bank; retail crypto trading + stablecoin-issuer banking.
entities.story: First Hong Kong virtual bank live; first to open retail crypto trading and to bank stablecoin issuers.
entities.licence: HK virtual bank; SFC-aligned crypto services

entities.name: Up
entities.story: Built by a tiny Melbourne studio; consistently Australia's most-loved banking app.

entities.name: Hnry
entities.story: Flat-fee 'never think about tax again' banking for NZ/AU sole traders.

entities.name: TymeBank
entities.story: Kiosk onboarding inside Pick n Pay stores cracked mass-market South African banking; now exporting the model to Asia.

entities.name: Bank Zero
entities.story: Ex-FNB CEO's zero-fee mutual bank built on its own core stack.

entities.name: Kuda
entities.note: 'Bank of the free'; NG + UK remittance.
entities.story: 'The bank of the free' — Nigeria's most-funded consumer neobank, now bridging NG↔UK remittance.

entities.name: OPay
entities.story: Opera's super-app bet on Nigeria; 60M users via a massive street-agent network.

entities.name: Moniepoint
entities.story: Started as agent-banking infrastructure, became the bank behind millions of Nigerian small businesses.

entities.name: FairMoney
entities.founders: Laurin Hainy, Matthieu Gendreau, Nicolas Berthozat

entities.name: Umba
entities.note: NG + KE digital bank (acquired MFB licence).

entities.name: Djamo
entities.story: YC's first Ivorian startup; banking francophone West Africa's mobile-money generation.

entities.name: Wave
entities.story: Cut Senegal's mobile-money fees by ~70% with a Sendwave spin-out; francophone Africa's first unicorn.

entities.name: Flouci
entities.note: Tunisia's digital-banking super-app; BCT sandbox; 250K+ accounts.
entities.story: Tunisia's financial super-app by Kaoun — free bank account + wallet with the first e-KYC and home-delivered card in the country.
entities.licence: Partner-bank model — BCT regulatory sandbox (1st cohort, 2020); not a bank
entities.founders: Nebras Jemel, Anis Kallel, Rostom Bouazizi (Kaoun)

entities.name: Telda
entities.story: Founded by Swvl's ex-CTO; Egypt's first Gen-Z money app.

entities.name: Zand
entities.note: First UAE all-digital bank licence; crypto-friendly corporate banking.
entities.story: First fully digital UAE bank licence; corporate arm banks the Gulf's crypto companies.

entities.name: YAP
entities.note: UAE's first independent digital banking app.

entities.name: NOW Money
entities.note: Accounts for migrant workers in the Gulf.
entities.story: Payroll accounts for Gulf migrant workers excluded from traditional banks.

entities.name: Sarwa
entities.founders: Mark Chahwan, Jad Sayegh, Nadine Mezher

entities.name: STC Bank
entities.note: From STC Pay to full digital bank.

entities.name: One Zero
entities.note: Israel's first new bank in 40+ years; AI banker.
entities.story: Israel's first new bank in 40+ years, built around an AI private banker.

entities.name: Pride Bank
entities.note: Digital bank for Brazil's LGBTQ+ community.
entities.story: Brazil's digital bank for the LGBTQ+ community, born from exclusion at traditional branches.

entities.name: Revolut
entities.note: 50M+ users; crypto trading; stablecoin in FCA sandbox 2026.
entities.story: From a fee-free FX card in Canary Wharf to a 50M-user global super-app; the UK licence took nine years.

entities.name: Cash App
entities.story: Square's P2P experiment became Block's consumer bank and America's biggest retail bitcoin on-ramp.

entities.name: Robinhood
entities.note: Broker + banking + crypto; USDG consortium member.
entities.licence: Broker-dealer + partner banks; state crypto licences

entities.name: Crypto.com
entities.story: Rebranded from Monaco after buying the crypto.com domain; the arena-naming-rights era of CeFi.
entities.licence: MiCA CASP, MAS MPI, US MSBs

entities.name: Coinbase Card
entities.note: Exchange card + new Amex One Card; Base builder.

entities.name: OKX Card
entities.note: Best rates for active OKX users.

entities.name: Bitpanda
entities.note: Stocks + crypto + metals broker with card.
entities.founders: Eric Demuth, Paul Klanschek, Christian Trummer

entities.name: eToro Money
entities.founders: Yoni Assia, Ronen Assia, David Ring

entities.name: Juno
entities.founders: Varun Deshpande, Ratnesh Ray, Siddharth Verma

entities.name: Strike
entities.story: Chicago Lightning evangelist turning bitcoin rails into invisible remittance infrastructure.

entities.name: Xapo Bank
entities.note: Licensed private bank for BTC wealth.
entities.story: Casares — patient zero of Silicon Valley bitcoin — built a private bank for BTC wealth after his family lost everything to Argentine devaluations.

entities.name: Nexo
entities.note: Crypto credit lines; rewards in credit mode.

entities.name: Krak
entities.story: Kraken's move from exchange to money app: spend, yield and P2P Kraktags on one balance.

entities.name: KAST
entities.story: Stablecoin-native neobank for the dollar-hungry global middle class; premium metal tiers, HK roots.

entities.name: RedotPay
entities.note: Processed $2.95B card volume in 2025 — over 4x its next 13 competitors combined.
entities.story: Hong Kong crypto-card leader: $2.95B card volume in 2025, more than 4x its next 13 competitors combined (CoinDesk/StraitsX).

entities.name: Kolo
entities.note: Zero-fee stablecoin card in 170+ countries; nomad favourite.

entities.name: Lava
entities.note: Bitcoin line of credit + card; dropped self-custody (DLC) for cold-storage custody in Sept 2025.
entities.story: Started as self-custodial DLC loans, pivoted to custody after a $200M raise — the loudest custody U-turn in bitcoin fintech.
entities.licence: Custodial (cold storage, distributed keys) since Sept 2025

entities.name: COCA
entities.note: MPC self-banking app: EUR IBAN + card, 75 countries; Wirex-issued cards.
entities.story: 1M+ users in 75 countries; 'self-banking' — EUR IBAN and card on a wallet only the user controls.

entities.name: Mercado Pago
entities.note: LatAm super-app; crypto BR/MX; Meli Dollar.
entities.story: MercadoLibre's payments arm became LatAm's default wallet; issues the Meli Dollar stable token in Brazil.
entities.licence: Payment/fintech licences region-wide; pursuing MX bank licence

entities.name: Lemon
entities.story: Argentina's crypto card leader, born in a Patagonian town, scaled on stablecoin savings culture.

entities.name: Bitso
entities.story: Mexico's first crypto unicorn; powers a meaningful share of US–MX remittances over stablecoin rails.
entities.founders: Daniel Vogel, Pablo González, Ben Peters

entities.name: DolarApp
entities.story: Digital-dollar accounts (USDc) that let LatAm users escape peso volatility with a card.

entities.name: Littio
entities.licence: On/off-ramp + issuer partners; USD via onchain treasuries

entities.name: Félix
entities.story: Send dollars home over WhatsApp; stablecoin rails under a chat interface.

entities.name: Chipper Cash
entities.story: Ugandan-Ghanaian founders built pan-African P2P; survived the FTX-investor winter and refocused on cards + crypto.

entities.name: Yellow Card
entities.story: Two Auburn students' bitcoin kiosk became Africa's largest stablecoin on/off-ramp.

entities.name: Deel
entities.story: Payroll for 1.5M global workers; issued its own DLUSD stablecoin on Stripe's Bridge + Privy + Tempo stack.

entities.name: Meow
entities.note: SMB banking born from crypto treasuries.

entities.name: Dakota
entities.story: Business banking where deposits sit in tokenized T-bills onchain — a bank without a bank.

entities.name: MetaMask
entities.note: Default self-custodial wallet; Agent Wallet: Blockaid scanning, tx simulation, MEV protection.
entities.story: Born inside Consensys in 2016, the wallet that onboarded a generation to Ethereum — now shipping mUSD, the Card, and Agent Wallet for autonomous finance.
entities.licence: Self-custodial software — no banking licence needed; card via licensed issuer partners

entities.name: Phantom
entities.founders: Brandon Millman, Chris Kalani, Francesco Agosti

entities.name: Solflare
entities.licence: Self-custodial software; card via issuer partners

entities.name: Rainbow
entities.founders: Mike Demarais, Christian Baroni, Jin Chung

entities.name: Payy
entities.story: Private-by-default stablecoin payments: balances hidden with zero-knowledge proofs, spendable on a Visa.
entities.licence: Self-custodial ZK L2; card via issuer partners

entities.name: Gnosis Pay
entities.note: Card wired to your own Safe on Gnosis Chain.
entities.story: The first onchain bank account: a Visa card wired directly to a Safe smart account on Gnosis Chain.
entities.licence: E-money via Monavate (UK/EU); funds in your own Safe

entities.name: Holyheld
entities.note: IBAN + card on your external wallet; gasless.

entities.name: EtherFi Cash
entities.story: Borrow against restaked ETH at the point of sale — credit without selling your stack.

volume.metric: annual card spend · 2025 · top Dune cohort

entities.name: Avici
entities.note: Self-custody neobank with full Visa access.

entities.name: Bleap
entities.note: MPC key-sharding; zero FX; free ATM tier.

entities.name: Tuyo
entities.note: USDC on Base for EUR/USD/MXN corridors.

entities.name: Ready
entities.story: Argent pioneered smart-account wallets and social recovery; rebranded to Ready to become an onchain bank.

entities.name: Plasma One
entities.note: Stablecoin neobank on own L1; XPL −94% from high.
entities.story: A stablecoin-native neobank running on its own chain — zero-fee USD₮ transfers, though XPL fell ~94% from its high.
entities.licence: Own L1; card via issuer partners

entities.name: Karta
entities.note: Telegram-first self-custody card (Rain-issued); settles on Tempo; no US.
entities.story: 25K+ monthly cardholders spending from their own wallet, reconciled on Tempo in under a second.
entities.licence: Self-custodial (MPC via Privy); Visa issued by Rain

entities.name: Hyperbeat
entities.note: Liquid Banking on Hyperliquid: spend cash or borrow at point of sale via Morpho.
entities.story: Banking for the Hyperliquid economy: trade, earn and spend one balance — credit mode borrows via Morpho at the terminal.
entities.licence: Self-custodial smart accounts (HyperEVM); Paxos Labs stablecoin infra

entities.name: Cypher
entities.note: Multichain card; also powers Moonwell Card.

entities.name: Exa
entities.note: DeFi credit + debit on Exactly protocol.

entities.name: MiniPay
entities.note: Opera's Celo wallet; 10M+; PIX/Mercado Pago rails.
entities.story: Opera's 2MB stablecoin wallet for the Global South: 10M+ wallets, PIX and Mercado Pago rails, USSD-light design.

entities.name: El Dorado
entities.story: USDT P2P marketplace that became Venezuela's parallel dollar bank, expanding across the Andean region.

entities.name: Oobit
entities.note: Non-custodial pass-through; Tether-backed; Agent Cards for AI agents.
entities.story: Tap-to-pay from your own wallet; first corporate crypto card for AI agents (Agent Cards).

entities.name: Sling Money
entities.story: Ex-Monzo/Facebook team making stablecoin P2P feel like iMessage, on Solana.

entities.name: Deblock
entities.story: A French-licensed current account where the crypto side stays in your own non-custodial wallet — the clearest mixed-custody design in Europe.

entities.name: Fiat24
entities.note: Swiss-licensed; your account is an NFT on Arbitrum.
entities.story: Your bank account is literally an NFT on Arbitrum; Swiss-regulated, onchain-settled.

entities.name: Infini
entities.story: Stablecoin card that survived a $49M private-key exploit in Feb 2025 and rebuilt.

entities.name: Moon
entities.note: Privacy-lean virtual cards funded with crypto.

entities.name: WeFi
entities.story: Deobank thesis: deposits never leave user-controlled onchain accounts.

entities.name: UglyCash
entities.note: LatAm dollar app on stablecoin rails.

entities.name: Onboard
entities.story: Self-custody 'deobank' thesis from Lagos: your keys, your dollars, your card.

entities.name: Yuh
entities.note: Swissquote x PostFinance app; custodial crypto trading.

entities.name: Pixpay
entities.founders: Benoit Grassin, Nicolas Klein, Caroline Ménager

entities.name: Pomelo
entities.note: Send money home via credit; US↔PH corridor.
entities.story: Turns remittances into credit-building: send money home on a card, build US credit history.

entities.story: Open-source USDC wallet born from Devconnect payments frustration; passkey-secured, no seed phrase.

entities.note: Saudi Arabia's third licensed digital bank.

entities.note: USDC neobank for Venezuela + LatAm corridors.

entities.name: Rizon
entities.note: Stablecoin banking app on self-custody rails.

entities.note: Stablecoin business banking for global SMBs.

entities.note: Neobank for African diaspora in Europe.

entities.name: Baraka
entities.note: MENA retail investing app expanding to money features.

entities.name: GCash
entities.note: The Philippines' super-app wallet; ~94M users, GCredit + GSave make it bank-like.

entities.name: PhonePe
entities.note: India's UPI giant — 600M+ registered; wallets, deposits marketplace, insurance.

entities.name: Paytm
entities.note: India's OG super-app wallet; payments bank wound down, wallet + UPI live on.

entities.name: GrabPay
entities.note: SEA super-app wallet across 8 countries; sibling of GXS Bank.

entities.name: GoPay
entities.note: GoTo's wallet; Indonesia's ride-hail-born money app, Jago-partnered.

entities.name: OVO
entities.note: Grab-backed Indonesian e-wallet with investing + lending layers.

entities.name: TrueMoney
entities.note: CP Group's wallet across 7 SEA markets; Thailand's biggest.

entities.name: MoMo

entities.note: VNG's wallet riding Zalo, Vietnam's biggest chat app.

entities.note: Malaysia's toll-born wallet turned everyday money app; Ant-backed.
